---
title: Templates
---

Every piece of output in *Calepin* is controlled by a template: element markup, page wrappers, collection layouts. Templates use [MiniJinja](https://github.com/mitsuhiko/minijinja) syntax (Jinja2 for Rust). Learn one syntax and it works everywhere.


# Jinja syntax

Insert values with double braces. Dot notation accesses nested fields:

```
{{ meta.title }}
{{ var.urls.docs }}
```

Functions take keyword arguments:

```
{{ pagebreak() }}
{{ video(url="https://youtube.com/watch?v=abc") }}
```

Conditionals and loops:

```
{% if engine == "html" %}
HTML-only content.
{% endif %}

{% for item in var.features %}
- {{ item }}
{% endfor %}
```

Filters transform values with pipes: {% raw %}`{{ meta.title | upper }}`{% endraw %}.

Comments: {% raw %}`{# ignored #}`{% endraw %}. Raw blocks: {% raw %}`{% raw %}...{% endraw %}`{% endraw %}. Literal braces in LaTeX templates: {% raw %}`{{_lb}}` and `{{_rb}}`{% endraw %}.


# Where templates are used

## Document body

The `.qmd` body is processed as a Jinja template. Code blocks and inline code are protected from evaluation. Available context:

| Variable | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| {% raw %}`{{ meta.title }}`{% endraw %}, {% raw %}`{{ meta.author }}`{% endraw %}, ... | YAML front matter fields |
| {% raw %}`{{ var.key }}`{% endraw %} | Custom front matter fields (with nesting) |
| {% raw %}`{{ env.HOME }}`{% endraw %} | System environment variables |
| {% raw %}`{{ engine }}`{% endraw %} | Rendering engine: `html`, `latex`, `typst`, `markdown` |
| {% raw %}`{{ target }}`{% endraw %} | Target name (defaults to engine name) |
| {% raw %}`{% include "name" %}`{% endraw %} | Reusable content from `_calepin/partials/` |

Built-in functions: `pagebreak()`, `video()`, `brand()`, `lipsum()`, `placeholder()`. Plugins can register additional functions via the shortcode capability.


## Element templates

Every rendered element (divs, spans, code blocks, figures, theorems, callouts) passes through a template. Templates are named `{element}.{format}` (e.g., `callout.html`, `theorem.latex`).

Div variables: {% raw %}`{{children}}`{% endraw %}, {% raw %}`{{classes}}`{% endraw %}, {% raw %}`{{id}}`{% endraw %}, {% raw %}`{{id_attr}}`{% endraw %}, plus any user attributes from the div header.

Span variables: {% raw %}`{{content}}`{% endraw %}, {% raw %}`{{class}}`{% endraw %}, {% raw %}`{{classes}}`{% endraw %}, {% raw %}`{{id}}`{% endraw %}, plus user attributes.

Code variables: {% raw %}`{{code}}`{% endraw %}, {% raw %}`{{lang}}`{% endraw %}, {% raw %}`{{highlighted}}`{% endraw %}, {% raw %}`{{label}}`{% endraw %}.

Figure variables: {% raw %}`{{image}}`{% endraw %}, {% raw %}`{{caption}}`{% endraw %}, {% raw %}`{{number}}`{% endraw %}, {% raw %}`{{label}}`{% endraw %}, {% raw %}`{{width_attr}}`{% endraw %}, {% raw %}`{{align}}`{% endraw %}.

To override any element, drop a file in `_calepin/templates/{engine}/` (e.g., `templates/html/callout.html`). Hyphens in class names are normalized to underscores for lookup.

### Example

Create `_calepin/templates/html/highlight_box.html`:

```html
<div class="highlight-box" style="background: {{color}};
     border-left: 4px solid #333; padding: 0.8em 1em;">
<strong>{{title}}</strong>
{{children}}
</div>
```

Use as:

    ::: {.highlight-box title="Key takeaway" color="#e8f4f8"}
    Templates are the simplest way to customize *Calepin*.
    :::


## Page templates

The page template wraps the rendered body in a document shell: the HTML `<head>`, the LaTeX preamble, or the Typst equivalent. Override with `_calepin/templates/{engine}/page.{ext}`.

Key variables: {% raw %}`{{body}}`{% endraw %}, {% raw %}`{{toc}}`{% endraw %}, {% raw %}`{{plain_title}}`{% endraw %}, {% raw %}`{{title}}`{% endraw %}, {% raw %}`{{author}}`{% endraw %}, {% raw %}`{{date}}`{% endraw %}, {% raw %}`{{css}}`{% endraw %} (HTML), {% raw %}`{{preamble}}`{% endraw %}.

Pre-rendered blocks (each produced by an overridable element template): {% raw %}`{{title}}`{% endraw %}, {% raw %}`{{authors}}`{% endraw %}, {% raw %}`{{date}}`{% endraw %}, {% raw %}`{{abstract}}`{% endraw %}, {% raw %}`{{appendix}}`{% endraw %}, {% raw %}`{{bibliography}}`{% endraw %}.

Custom front matter fields are also available: `custom_footer: "Confidential"` in YAML becomes {% raw %}`{{custom_footer}}`{% endraw %} in the page template.


# Resolution order

All template lookups follow the same chain. The first match wins:

1. **Plugin** `elements/` or `templates/` directory (in plugin declaration order)
2. **User** `_calepin/templates/{target}/` (target-specific, e.g., `website/`)
3. **User** `_calepin/templates/{engine}/` (engine-specific, e.g., `html/`)
4. **User** `_calepin/templates/common/` (format-agnostic `.jinja`)
5. **Theme** templates (same three-layer lookup: target, engine, common)
6. **Built-in** (compiled into the binary)

The same order applies to snippets under `_calepin/snippets/`.

User files always win over plugins and themes. Plugins override themes. See [Themes](themes.html) for the theme layer and [Plugins](../misc/plugins.html) for plugin template overrides.


# Built-in element templates

| Template | Used by |
|----------|---------|
| `div` | Generic fenced divs |
| `code_source` | Code chunks (source) |
| `code_output` | Code chunks (output) |
| `code_diagnostic` | Warnings, messages, errors |
| `figure` | Figures and images |
| `callout` | All callout types |
| `theorem_italic` | theorem, lemma, corollary, proposition, conjecture |
| `theorem_normal` | definition, example, exercise, solution, remark, algorithm |
| `proof` | Proof environments |
| `title` | Document title |
| `authors` | Author metadata |
| `date` | Document date |
| `abstract` | Abstract text |
| `bibliography` | Bibliography section |
| `appendix` | Appendix wrapper |
